_note: Ground truth skill annotations for 9 evaluation resumes against the Ema Software Engineering Lead ML JD (swe_jd_ema.txt). Candidate IDs match filename stems as produced by RunSaver slugification. Annotated directly from resume text — explicit skills from dedicated skills sections only, implicit skills from work history evidence.

_annotation_guide.explicit_skills: Skills listed in a dedicated Skills / Technical Skills / Core Competencies / Core Skills section. Normalized to 1-4 word atomic tokens. Not inferred — only what appears in that section.
_annotation_guide.implicit_skills: Skills clearly demonstrated in work history, projects, or research — NOT from a skills section. Strong evidence only: appears across 2+ roles, central to a job title, or has quantified outcomes. Vague adjacency excluded.
_annotation_guide.candidate_id_note: IDs match the filename stem (e.g. R1_Arjun_Mehta_SeniorMLE). RunSaver lowercases and replaces spaces/hyphens with underscores when creating candidate folders.

R1_Arjun_Mehta_SeniorMLE._description: Staff MLE at Cohere, 7+ YoE, deep LLM/RAG/agent background, IIT+CMU, publications. Strong match on almost every required skill.

_annotation_notes.retrieval_ranking: Explicitly listed bi-encoder/cross-encoder ranking in skills; RAG platform built in work history.
_annotation_notes.rl: PPO-based reward model at LinkedIn — strong implicit evidence.
_annotation_notes.agents: Multi-agent reasoning system with LangGraph at Cohere — explicit and implicit.
_annotation_notes.distributed_training: Fine-tuned LLaMA-2 13B with FSDP across 32 A100s — listed in skills and evidenced in work history.

R2_Dr_Priya_Venkataraman_AcademicCV._description: Stanford PhD + postdoc, 6 publications including ACL/EMNLP, deep expertise in RAG, retrieval, ranking, RLHF, dialogue. Strong research credentials, limited production MLOps experience.

_annotation_notes.rlhf: RLHF is in her research interests and postdoc focus — strong implicit evidence.
_annotation_notes.production_gap: No explicit MLOps, model deployment, ETL, or A/B testing in resume — correctly excluded from both lists.
_annotation_notes.dialog: Dissertation is on grounded dialogue via RAG — strong implicit evidence.
_annotation_notes.model_training: PhD research on retrieval fine-tuning, contrastive pre-training at Google — demonstrated across multiple contexts.

R3_Sneha_Iyer_DataScientist._description: Senior Data Scientist at Target, 5 YoE, strong classical ML and data engineering, basic NLP only (TF-IDF, no transformers). No LLM/RAG/agent experience. Good A/B testing and SQL depth.

_annotation_notes.nlp_caveat: NLP is listed but explicitly qualified as 'basic' (TF-IDF, NLTK, TextBlob). The LLM skill matcher should flag this — she does NOT have transformer/LLM NLP. Listed here because it appears in skills section but annotated as a known weakness.
_annotation_notes.etl_implicit: dbt models, Airflow pipelines, Redshift — demonstrated across 2 roles.
_annotation_notes.model_deployment: Automated churn scoring pipeline via Airflow — implicit deployment evidence.

R4_Marcus_Chen_MLOps._description: Senior MLOps Engineer at Zillow, 7 YoE in platform/infra. Excellent MLOps, cloud, CI/CD depth. Deployed LLMs but did not train/design them. No NLP, no LLM training, no feature engineering, no retrieval/ranking/RL work.

_annotation_notes.pytorch_caveat: Listed as 'PyTorch (inference optimization)' — explicitly not training. The LLM matcher should be strict here per the JD's training/deployment requirement.
_annotation_notes.llm_serving_only: vLLM deployment of LLaMA-2 — serving only, explicitly noted in resume. Not counted as model training or NLP depth.
_annotation_notes.no_nlp_no_llm_design: No NLP, no transformer model design, no LLM training, no RAG — excluded.
_annotation_notes.no_rl_no_agents_no_retrieval: No evidence of any of these — excluded.
_annotation_notes.sql_implicit: Referenced in data engineering context across multiple roles — weak but present implicit signal.

R5_Jake_Thompson_BackendSWE._description: Senior Backend Engineer, 6 YoE, strong Python/Go services and infrastructure. No ML experience of any kind. Explicitly looking for backend/platform roles.

_annotation_notes.no_ml_anything: Zero ML, NLP, LLM, RAG, RL, agents, model training/deployment — all excluded.
_annotation_notes.elasticsearch_caveat: Listed 'Elasticsearch (search queries only)' — not retrieval systems in ML sense. Excluded.
_annotation_notes.data_structures_algorithms: Mentioned in summary and evidenced in backend work — listed as preferred skill match.
_annotation_notes.cloud_platforms: AWS and GCP listed explicitly in skills section.

business_analyst_resume_enhanced._description: Business Analyst at Accenture/Infosys, 4 YoE. SQL and Python for reporting/dashboards. No ML depth, no NLP, no LLMs. One-line churn model mention in projects with no detail.

_annotation_notes.churn_model: Mentioned as a project achievement with no detail on method, framework, or outcome — insufficient evidence for 'machine learning' implicit skill.
_annotation_notes.power_bi_tableau: BI tools — not ML relevant for this JD.

frontend_developer_resume_enhanced._description: Frontend Engineer at Swiggy/Zomato, 4 YoE. React/TypeScript specialist. No overlap with ML JD requirements.

_annotation_notes.python: Not actually listed — Core Skills are JS/React/TS. Excluded on closer reading.
_annotation_notes.correction: No Python listed — explicit_skills should be empty.
_annotation_notes.no_ml_anything: Entirely frontend — zero overlap with JD requirements.

management_consultant_resume_enhanced._description: Senior Consultant at Deloitte/EY-Parthenon. Strategy, financial modeling, market research. No technical ML skills.

_annotation_notes.no_ml_anything: Financial modeling and strategy work — no ML, no NLP, no Python, no LLMs.
_annotation_notes.analytics_dashboard: Mentioned as an achievement but no technical detail — not counted as an ML skill.

product_manager_resume_enhanced._description: Product Manager at Flipkart/Paytm. A/B testing and SQL used in product context, not ML context. No technical ML background.

_annotation_notes.ab_testing: Explicitly listed in Core Skills and evidenced in work history — included as explicit.
_annotation_notes.recommendation_feature: Launched recommendation feature — product ownership, not ML implementation. Not counted.
_annotation_notes.no_ml_nlp_llm: No technical ML implementation anywhere — excluded.
